MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-30-2021 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-26-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-11-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 437c(p)(2).)

A "dangerous condition" of public property is "a condition of property that creates a substantial (as distinguished from minor, trivial, or insignificant) risk of injury when . . . used with due care in a manner in which it is reasonably foreseeable that it will be used." (Gov. Code sec. 830(a).) "The existence of a dangerous condition ordinarily is a question of fact . . . but it can be decided as a matter of law if reasonable minds can come to only one conclusion." (Bonanno v. Central Contra Costa Transit Authority (2003) 30 Cal.4th 139, 148.) Here, defendant fails to show as a matter of law that the unmarked former driveway in the middle of the sidewalk is not a dangerous condition of public property. (See Sims Decl., Ex. B (Exs. A8, A10, A11 to Plaintiff's Depo.).) (See Stathoulis v. City of Montebello (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 559, 566-570 [reversing summary judgment for city where trial court erred in finding that gouge marks and potholes in street constituted a trivial defect as a matter of law].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
